dev.jeka.core.api.testing

Class JkTestProcessor

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.testing.JkTestProcessor

  • public final class JkTestProcessor
extends java.lang.Object
    Processor executing a given bunch of tests existing in compiled Java classes.

    This relies on Junit-platform (junit5), but having Junit-platform libraries on the classpath is optional. For most of the cases, processors can be used without coding against junit-platform API but, it is possible to use directly Junit-platform API for fine-tuning.

    Users can configure

    • Runtime support : classpath, forked process
    • Engine behavior : listeners, reports, progress display
    • The tests to run: discovery, selectors, filters, ...

    • Field Detail

      • engineBehavior

        public final JkTestProcessor.JkEngineBehavior engineBehavior

      • preActions

        public final JkRunnables preActions

      • postActions

        public final JkRunnables postActions
        Collection of Runnables to be executed after the test processor has run.

        It is typically used for generating reports after a test definition has run.

        If you want to run another test 'suite', you'll need to use JkProjectTesting.postActions instead.

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public JkTestProcessor setForkingProcess(boolean fork)

        public JkTestProcessor setRepoSetSupplier(java.util.function.Supplier<JkRepoSet> repoSetSupplier)

      • run

        public JkTestResult run(JkTestSelection testSelection)
        Launches the specified test set with the underlying junit-platform. The classloader running the tests includes the classpath of the current classloader plus the specified one.

      • runMatchingPatterns

        public JkTestResult runMatchingPatterns(java.lang.String... patterns)
        Launches the specified test set by including the provided patterns, using the underlying junit-platform setup. The method processes the given inclusion patterns to determine which tests to run and executes them.
        See Also:
        run(JkTestSelection), JkTestSelection.IT_PATTERN, JkTestSelection.E2E_PATTERN

      • launch

        @Deprecated
public JkTestResult launch()
        Deprecated. Use #run instead

      • run

        public JkTestResult run()
        Launches the default test selection using the underlying JUnit Platform setup. This method executes tests defined within the provided test classpath and root directories.

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
        Non-public API. Used by #launchInClassloader.
